element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Multiple modes of play across time periods

typical_tension_triggers: Time period shifts requiring navigation adaptation
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Multiple gameplay modes with variable pacing across different time periods

difficulty_curve.early_game: Basic platforming introduction with simple enemy avoidance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy density and introduction of time period navigation complexity
notable_spikes: Transitions between 1955, 1985, and future time periods introduce new spatial layouts

notable_level_patterns.description: Game features multiple modes of play with ability to travel between different time periods (1955, 1985, future). Each period presents distinct level layouts and platforming challenges
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple time period levels throughout game

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard side-scrolling horizontal progression with enemy encounters and platform navigation

extraction_notes.limitations: Available sources provide minimal level design detail. TCRF source focused on technical oddities (300 BPM music tempo) and build info. Wikipedia source lists game as 'multiple modes of play' and mentions time period transitions but lacks sequencing specifics. Direct gameplay documentation unavailable.
extraction_notes.inference_confidence: Low - extracted structure based on game description as side-scrolling platformer with time-period mechanics. Specific element introduction order, tension cycles, and spatial patterns inferred from paradigm conventions, not documented sources.
